headline: Pertamina Geothermal Energy and ITB study CCS deployment at a geothermal field
summary: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy (PGEO) and Institut Teknologi Bandung (ITB) are studying the feasibility of deploying carbon capture and storage at an Indonesian geothermal field, an early-stage R&D collaboration exploring CO2 management/reinjection in geothermal reservoirs.
co2crc_note: APAC CCS R&D from an Indonesian state operator/university pairing; CCS-at-geothermal is a novel storage angle and Indonesia is a growing regional storage market CO2CRC engages with (Otway hosts SE-Asian delegations).
extractor_note: Feasibility/R&D study surfaced via Google Alerts (Petromindo); full article not fetchable (proxy 403), summary from alert headline + trade context.

headline: UK's Airhive acquires Dutch rival Carbyon to build a larger European DAC company
summary: London-based direct-air-capture startup Airhive acquired Dutch competitor Carbyon in an all-consolidation deal (terms undisclosed) it says creates one of Europe's biggest integrated DAC companies, combining Airhive's fluidised-bed approach with Carbyon's high-kinetics solid sorbent; teams merge under the Airhive name with early R&D concentrated at High Tech Campus Eindhoven on a next-gen 'Cascade' technology. Airhive framed the move as consolidation amid uncertain DAC demand and policy support.
co2crc_note: Signals consolidation pressure in the engineered-CDR/DAC sector as demand and policy support wobble — a market-structure signal for the CDR credits CCS players also compete in.
extractor_note: M&A between DAC startups; terms undisclosed so amount null. Cited Bloomberg (paywall); corroborated by Dutch trade press (industrielinqs, IO+).

headline: Microsoft's emissions jump 25% on AI buildout as it reaffirms carbon-removal commitment
summary: Microsoft's 2026 sustainability report shows FY2025 emissions of ~20 Mt CO2e, up 25% year-on-year and ~58% above its 2020 baseline, driven by AI data-centre expansion; the company reaffirmed its carbon-negative-by-2030 goal and one of the world's largest corporate CDR portfolios (29 projects added across 10 pathways, >45 Mt of removals contracted), even as its H1-2026 offtake volume fell 82% to ~9.5 Mt.
co2crc_note: Microsoft is the largest corporate buyer of engineered CDR/BECCS credits; rising demand-side pressure (and its offtake volatility) shapes the CCS/BECCS credit market Australian projects hope to sell into.
extractor_note: Corporate sustainability-report / CDR-demand item; cited Carbon Pulse (paywall), corroborated by edie, GeekWire, gasworld, Carbon Herald. No discrete new money commitment -> amount null, commitment_status na.
